title: A thin film composite hollow fibre membrane
abstract: There is provided a thin film composite hollow fibre membrane comprising a porous hollow fibre support layer formed of a suitable polymer and a selective layer on an outer circumferential surface of the hollow fibre support layer, the selective layer formed of a cross-linked polyamide, wherein the thin film composite hollow fibre membrane has a salt permeability of 0.028-0.042 L m -2 h -1 and a collapse pressure of at least 20 bar. There is also provided of making the thin film composite hollow fibre membrane.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A thin film composite hollow fibre membrane comprising: - a porous hollow fibre support layer formed of polyethersulfone, polysulfone, polyphenylsulfone, polyacrylonitrile, polyimide, polyether imide, polyamide-imde, polyvinylidene fluoride, cellulose triacetate, polyetherketone, polyetheretherketone or a combination thereof, the hollow fibre support layer having a thickness of 100-350 Î¼Î¹Î· ; and - a selective layer on an outer circumferential surface of the hollow fibre support layer, the selective layer formed of a cross-linked polyamide and having a thickness of 100-500 nm, wherein the thin film composite hollow fibre membrane has a salt permeability of 0.028-0.042 L m "2 h "1 and a collapse pressure of at least 20 bar. 2. The thin film composite hollow fibre membrane according to claim 1 , wherein the hollow fibre support layer is formed of polyethersulfone. 3. The thin film composite hollow fibre membrane according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the thin film composite hollow fibre membrane has a pure water permeability rate of 1.0-2.0 L m "2 h "1 bar "1 . 4. The thin film composite hollow fibre membrane according to any preceding claim, wherein the thin film composite hollow fibre membrane has a power density of 7.0-10.5 W m 2 . 5. The thin film composite hollow fibre membrane according to any preceding claim, wherein the thin film composite hollow fibre membrane has a salt rejection rate of 80-90%. 6. A method of preparing a thin film composite hollow fibre membrane as described in any of claims 1 to 5, the method comprising: - preparing a module comprising at least one porous hollow fibre support layer potted in the module; and - forming a selective layer on an outer circumferential surface of the hollow fibre support layer through interfacial polymerization, 

 wherein the forming comprises contacting the surface of the porous hollow fibre support layer with a first solution comprising a polyamine, removing excess of the first solution by applying vacuum and subsequently contacting the surface of the porous hollow fibre support layer with a second solution comprising a polyfunctional acyl halide. 7. The method according to claim 6, wherein the porous hollow fibre support layer is prepared by: providing a dope solution comprising a polymer solution, a solvent/non- solvent mixture and water to an annulus of a spinneret, the polymer solution comprising: polyethersulfone, polysulfone, polyphenylsulfone, polyacrylonitrile, polyimide, polyether imide, polyamide-imde, polyvinylidene fluoride, cellulose triacetate, polyetherketone, polyetheretherketone or a combination thereof; providing a bore solution to an inner tube of the spinneret; and extruding the dope solution and bore solution through the spinneret into a coagulation bath, thereby obtaining a porous hollow fibre support layer. 8. The method according to claim 6 or 7, wherein the vacuum is applied at a transmembrane pressure of 150-1000 mbar. 9. The method according to any of claims 6 to 8, wherein the first solution comprises a polyamine selected from the group consisting of: m-phenylenediamine (MPD), p-phenylenediamine, p-xylylenediamine, cyclohexanediamine, piperazine, branched or dendrimeric polyethylenimine, and a combination thereof. 10. The method according to any of claims 6 to 9, wherein the first solution further comprises a surfactant. 11. The method according to claim 10, wherein the surfactant is selected from the group consisting of: sodium dodecyl sulphate (SDS), trimethylamine (TEA), camphorsulfonic acid (CSA), and a combination thereof. 12. The method according to any of claims 6 to 11 , wherein the second solution comprises a polyfunctional acyl halide selected from the group consisting of: trimesoyi chloride (TMC), isophthaloyl chloride, terephthaloyl chloride, 1 ,3,5-cyclohexane tricarbonyl chloride, 1 ,2,3,4-cyclohexane tetracarbonyl chloride, and a combination thereof. 13. The method according to any of claims 6 to 12, wherein the second solution further comprises an organic solvent. 14. The method according to claim 13, wherein the organic solvent is selected from the group consisting of: hexane, heptane, and a combination thereof.
